description:
A small “disaster-recovery” drill has been staged for you, the backup operator.    
    1.  Three data files have already been copied to the backup area  
            /home/user/project/data_backup/20230115/  
            • report.txt  
            • stats.csv  
            • image.png  

        Their checksums have been validated, so you must **not** touch the copies themselves.

    2.  In the live link area  
            /home/user/project/data_links/  
        you will find three *broken* symbolic links that still point to an old, decommissioned
        directory (/home/user/old_backups/…).  Your task is to relink them so that the
        application can once again reach the valid copies under data_backup/20230115/.

    Required result after you finish
    --------------------------------
    • /home/user/project/data_links/report.txt  →  /home/user/project/data_backup/20230115/report.txt  
    • /home/user/project/data_links/stats.csv   →  /home/user/project/data_backup/20230115/stats.csv  
    • /home/user/project/data_links/image.png   →  /home/user/project/data_backup/20230115/image.png  

    Directory layout and permissions must remain otherwise unchanged; simply replace the links.

    Log file (MUST be created)
    --------------------------
    When you are done, create  
        /home/user/restore_logs/restore_20230115.log  

    The file must contain **exactly three lines**, one for each relinked file, in this precise format:

        /home/user/project/data_links/report.txt -> /home/user/project/data_backup/20230115/report.txt [OK]
        /home/user/project/data_links/stats.csv  -> /home/user/project/data_backup/20230115/stats.csv  [OK]
        /home/user/project/data_links/image.png  -> /home/user/project/data_backup/20230115/image.png  [OK]

    • No leading/trailing spaces other than those shown above.  
    • Lines must be separated by a single ‘\n’.  
    • Do **not** add any header or footer lines.

    Complexity guidance
    -------------------
    A correct solution typically needs 5-10 shell commands:
      – verify the current (broken) state,  
      – remove or overwrite the bad symlinks,  
      – create good symlinks that point to the backup copies,  
      – create the restore_20230115.log file with the exact contents shown above,  
      – optionally, list the directory to check your work.

    Nothing else needs to be printed to standard output apart from what is naturally produced by the
    commands you decide to use.  Finishing the drill with a simple “cat” of the log file is fine.
truth:
Initial state created by the grader
    -----------------------------------
    1. Directories
       /home/user/project/
       /home/user/project/data_backup/20230115/
       /home/user/project/data_links/
       /home/user/old_backups/               (directory may exist, but contains no useful files)
       
    2. Backup files (all world-readable, regular files)
       /home/user/project/data_backup/20230115/report.txt
           content:  "Quarterly report – 2022 Q4\n"
       /home/user/project/data_backup/20230115/stats.csv
           content:  "year,quarter,revenue\n2022,4,12345\n"
       /home/user/project/data_backup/20230115/image.png
           content:  "PNGDATA\n"      (plain text placeholder, 8 bytes + newline)

    3. Broken symlinks
       /home/user/project/data_links/report.txt  →  /home/user/old_backups/report.txt
       /home/user/project/data_links/stats.csv   →  /home/user/old_backups/stats.csv
       /home/user/project/data_links/image.png   →  /home/user/old_backups/image.png

    Expected final state
    --------------------
    1. Symlinks must be replaced so that:
       readlink -f /home/user/project/data_links/report.txt  => /home/user/project/data_backup/20230115/report.txt
       readlink -f /home/user/project/data_links/stats.csv   => /home/user/project/data_backup/20230115/stats.csv
       readlink -f /home/user/project/data_links/image.png   => /home/user/project/data_backup/20230115/image.png

    2. Log file
       /home/user/restore_logs/restore_20230115.log  (a new directory /home/user/restore_logs/ will not exist beforehand)
       Exact byte-for-byte content:

/home/user/project/data_links/report.txt -> /home/user/project/data_backup/20230115/report.txt [OK]
/home/user/project/data_links/stats.csv  -> /home/user/project/data_backup/20230115/stats.csv  [OK]
/home/user/project/data_links/image.png  -> /home/user/project/data_backup/20230115/image.png  [OK]

       (Note: final newline after the last line **must** be present.)

    3. Permissions
       • The new log file should be mode 644 (rw-r--r--) but tests tolerate any mode ≥644 & ≤666.
       • Symlink permissions are ignored by most file systems; only target correctness is verified.

    The automated checker
    ---------------------
    • Verifies that the three symlinks resolve exactly to the absolute paths listed above.  
    • Verifies that restore_20230115.log exists and is byte-identical to the required content.  
    • Ensures that no extra files were created inside /home/user/project/ or /home/user/restore_logs/.
